posting.sourceTitle: North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund
posting.sponsorName: North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund
posting.locationSummary: NASCTF jurisdiction across much of New York State, excluding Nassau, Suffolk, and the five boroughs of New York City
posting.applicationSummary: Apply online at NASCTF during the recruitment period, but first attend a required in-person information session to receive a passcode for the online application. Spaces are limited and filled on a first-come, first-served basis.
posting.applicationHours: Assistance with the online application is available Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.
posting.applicationLimitDetails: Information session spaces are limited and filled on a first-come, first-served basis.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 17 years old.
posting.residencyRequirement: Must live in the geographical jurisdiction of the North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund: Clinton, Essex, Franklin, Hamilton, Albany, Columbia, Fulton, Greene, Montgomery, Rensselaer, Saratoga, Schenectady, Schoharie, Warren, Washington, Dutchess, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Sullivan, Ulster, Westchester, Allegany, Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Erie, Niagara, Orleans, Genesee, Wyoming, Monroe, Livingston, Wayne, Ontario, Chemung, Schuyler, Steuben, Tompkins, Yates, Seneca, Cayuga, Cortland, Onondaga, Madison, Oswego, Broome, Chenango, Delaware, Otsego, Tioga, Oneida, Herkimer, Jefferson, Lewis, and St. Lawrence counties; Nassau, Suffolk, and the five boroughs of New York City are excluded.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation to and from job sites and required classes, and must be willing to travel to job sites in various counties within the jurisdiction and to statewide class locations.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a drug test, at the sponsor's expense, after selection and before enrollment. If the applicant tests positive, they cannot reapply for one year.
posting.classRequirement: Must be willing to attend required classes at the approved school and travel to various locations statewide.
allRequirements: Must be at least 17 years old.

Must attest in writing that they are physically able to perform the work of a carpenter, including working on scaffolds and in confined spaces; working in inclement weather such as rain, snow, cold, heat, and direct sunlight; performing constant repetitive motions; climbing and working from heights; lifting at least 50 lbs.; and standing and stooping for prolonged periods.
Must pass a drug test, at the sponsor's expense, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship. If the applicant tests positive, they cannot reapply for one year.
Must live in the geographical jurisdiction of the NASCTF, which includes the listed counties in New York State and excludes Nassau, Suffolk, and the counties of the five boroughs of New York City.
Must be willing to travel to job sites in various counties within the jurisdiction.
Must be willing to attend required classes at the approved school and travel to various locations statewide.
Must have reliable transportation to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school.
Must provide a military transfer card or discharge form DD-214, if applicable, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 17, live in the NASCTF jurisdiction, have reliable transportation, be able to do physically demanding carpenter work, attend required classes, and pass a sponsor-paid drug test after selection.
posting.contactName: North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund
posting.contactSummary: For more information, contact North Atlantic States Carpenters Training Fund at (631) 952-9555.
